title: Relapsed high-risk MDS with TP53 mutation and severe chronic GvHD in a 56-year-old female after alloSCT

patient.chief_complaint: Fever, dry cough, progressive fatigue, and worsening joint pain and stiffness
patient.vital_signs: BP 102/65 mmHg, HR 128/min, Temp 38.6 °C, RR 24/min, O₂ sat 87% (room air).

1.setting: Inpatient admission for acute respiratory symptoms in an immunocompromised post-transplant patient
1.capabilities: You are evaluating a clinically unstable patient with fever, hypoxemia, and respiratory symptoms in a hospital setting. You can obtain a focused history and examination, request routine blood evaluations, and arrange standard imaging appropriate for acute respiratory compromise. Invasive diagnostic procedures and complex analyses typically require further coordination.

2.setting: Inpatient Ward (Early Hematologic and Infectious Workup)
2.capabilities: The patient is hospitalized for further diagnostic clarification. You can organize invasive tissue sampling, a broader infectious workup, and detailed functional assessments. Interdisciplinary consultations can be requested. Some specialized test results may still be pending or require referral to external laboratories.

3.setting: Inpatient Ward / Post-transplant Relapse and GvHD Management Conference
3.capabilities: You now have access to the results of specialized tests with longer turnaround times, including molecular profiling, chimerism studies, and a complete longitudinal treatment history. You are expected to integrate these data with comorbidity and GvHD status to define prognosis and to formulate a comprehensive treatment strategy, including systemic therapy for relapse and management options for chronic GvHD.

demographics.valid_request_if: Requests for demographics or background characteristics such as age, sex, performance status, comorbidities, social situation, or anthropometrics.
demographics.content:
Age: 56 years
Sex: Female
Performance status: ECOG 1-2
Comorbidities: Chronic hepatitis B on antiviral prophylaxis, hypothyroidism, chronic GvHD (skin/lung), history of alloSCT, arterial hypertension, depression, CDK stage 3
Social: Divorced; lives alone, one adult daughter.
Height: 168 cm; Bodyweight: 61 kg.

history_physical.valid_request_if: Requests for history of present illness, review of systems, physical examination findings, or current medication list.
history_physical.content:
2025-04-04: The patient presented with fever (38.6 °C), dry cough, and progressive fatigue. She reported a 1-kg unintentional weight loss over the past three months and worsening joint pain and stiffness. Chest pain was denied. On examination, the patient appeared pale with sclerodermiform skin changes and retractions, whitish and irritated oral mucosa, and a cGvHD-typical exanthema characterized by lichenoid, erythematous lesions involving the trunk and extremities. Tachypnea at rest was noted. No neurological deficits were present.
Current medication: levothyroxine 75 µg 1-0-0, ramipril 5 mg 1-0-0, bisoprolol 2.5 mg 1-0-0, sertraline 100 mg 1-0-0, entecavir 0.5 mg 1-0-0, azithromycin 250 mg 3×/week, montelukast 10 mg 1-0-0, fluticasone inhaler 2x/day, valaciclovir 500 mg 1-0-0, cotrimoxazole forte 480 mg 1-0-0 (Mon/Wed/Fri), prednisolone 10 mg 1-0-0, isavuconazole 200 mg 1-0-0.

cbc_coagulation.valid_request_if: Requests for blood counts, coagulation parameters, or a general overview of cytopenias and clotting status.
cbc_coagulation.content: Hb 9.6 g/dL, WBC 2.8 × 10⁹/L, Plt 46 × 10⁹/L; ANC 1.2 × 10⁹/L, lymphocytes 1.0 × 10⁹/L, monocytes 0.5 × 10⁹/L; no peripheral blasts. INR 1.3, aPTT 33 s, fibrinogen 160 mg/dL, D-dimer 520 µg/L FEU.

chemistry_inflammation.valid_request_if: Requests for serum chemistry, markers of organ function, and inflammatory parameters.
chemistry_inflammation.content: Na 134 mmol/L, K 4.9 mmol/L, creatinine 1.7 mg/dL (eGFR 42 mL/min/1.73 m²), AST 132 U/L, ALT 98 U/L, bilirubin 1.7 mg/dL, LDH 351 U/L, uric acid 7.9 mg/dL, CRP 5.1 mg/L. Troponin T 0.012 ng/mL.

imaging_chest_ct.valid_request_if: Requests for cross-sectional imaging of the chest such as CT for evaluation of pulmonary infiltrates.
imaging_chest_ct.content: Chest CT (2025-04-05): Diffuse bilateral ground-glass opacities with one nodular lesion showing a halo sign in the right upper lobe. Additional findings suggestive of BOOP with patchy, peripheral, and peribronchovascular consolidations in a migratory pattern.

imaging_abdominal_ultrasound.valid_request_if: Requests for abdominal ultrasound or sonography of liver and spleen.
imaging_abdominal_ultrasound.content: Abdominal ultrasound (2025-04-05): Hepatosplenomegaly with multiple hypoechoic splenic lesions, consistent with hemangiomas.

echocardiography.valid_request_if: Requests for transthoracic echocardiography or cardiac ultrasound findings.
echocardiography.content: Echocardiogram (2025-04-05): Left ventricular ejection fraction (LVEF) 45%, mild pulmonary hypertension (RVSP 45 mmHg).

bone_marrow.valid_request_if: Requests for bone marrow morphology or flow cytometry to evaluate blasts and hematopoiesis.
bone_marrow.content: Flow cytometry revealed 6% blasts, expressing CD34+, CD117+, CD13+, CD38+, CD123+, and CD45dim. Negative for HLA-DR and CD33. Aberrant co-expression of CD7. Negative for CD19, CD20, CD3. Cytomorphology showed a hypocellular marrow (25%) with mild reactive-toxic changes including vacuolization and cytoplasmic granulation. Erythropoiesis and granulopoiesis were markedly reduced, and megakaryopoiesis was sparse, consisting mainly of juvenile forms with nuclear atypia. Approximately 5–10% large blasts with prominent nucleoli and dark cytoplasm were observed. Macrophages were increased.

cytogenetics.valid_request_if: Requests for conventional cytogenetic analysis or karyotype.

infectious_workup_and_pulmonary_function.valid_request_if: Requests for extended infectious diagnostics, fungal markers, bronchoalveolar lavage results, or pulmonary function testing.
infectious_workup_and_pulmonary_function.content: Serum galactomannan 1.1; β-D-glucan and blood cultures negative were negative. PCR from the peripheral blood was negative for EBV, CMV and ADV. Pulmonary function testing demonstrated a markedly reduced diffusion capacity (DLCO 62% predicted) and a moderate obstructive component (FEV₁ 56% predicted). Bronchoalveolar lavage grew ESBL-producing Klebsiella pneumoniae harboring an OXA-48 carbapenemase gene, and was PCR-positive for influenza A (Ct 17). Antimicrobial susceptibility testing showed resistance to piperacillin/tazobactam, all cephalosporins, and carbapenems (imipenem, meropenem, ertapenem). Last-resort antibiotics results were pending. Additionally, BAL galactomannan was elevated at 2.1. Serum ferritin was 2380 µg/L, PCT 1.24 ng/mL.

molecular_panel.valid_request_if: Requests for molecular profiling or next-generation sequencing results of the hematologic disease.
molecular_panel.content: Mutations detected: TP53 (VAF 7%), SF3B1 (VAF 12%), and DNMT3A (VAF 3%). No FLT3-ITD/TKD, NPM1, CEBPA, or IDH1/2 mutations were found. Chimerism analysis revealed 4.7% recipient origin

mrd_status.valid_request_if: Requests for measurable residual disease status, including chimerism and leukemia-associated immunophenotype.
mrd_status.content: Relapsed disease with persistent recipient chimerism (1.7%). Flow: CD34+, CD117+, CD13+, CD38+, CD123+, and CD45dim. TP53, SF3B1, DNMT3A mutations & complex karyotype.

treatment_history.valid_request_if: Requests for prior MDS diagnosis, transplant course, GvHD history, and previous therapies.
treatment_history.content:
2021-02-06: Diagnosis of MDS-MLD (WHO 2016). Cytogenetic analysis showed a complex karyotype. Molecular genetics revealed SF3B1 and DNMT3A mutations. Initial bone marrow: 3% blasts. Laboratory findings: anemia (Hb 8.0 g/dL) and thrombocytopenia (platelets approximately 100 ×10⁹/L). IPSS-R: 6 points (high). IPSS-M: 1.03 (high). Initial treatment: Erythropoietin (EPO) and referral to a transplant center with donor search initiated. 2022-02: Allogeneic stem cell transplantation was performed from an HLA-C–mismatched donor. Conditioning included fludarabine, treosulfan, thiotepa, and post-transplant cyclophosphamide. 2022-04: Developed acute intestinal GvHD grade III (overall severity grade III). 2022-04-13: Rectoscopy revealed focal discrete inflammation at the rectosigmoid junction. Histology: sigmoid/rectum biopsy confirmed GvHD grade 3 (Snover et al.).
Prednisolone 2 mg/kg (120 mg/day) was started on 2022-04-09, plus topical budesonide 3 x 3 mg daily since 2022-04-11. Ruxolitinib 10 mg BID initiated on 2022-04-17 due to steroid-refractory aGvHD of the gut. Two doses of etanercept 25 mg s.c. were given on 2022-04-17 and 2022-04-23.
Infectious causes of diarrhea were excluded via stool bacteriology and virology on 2022-04-09 and repeated on 2022-04-25. Off-label therapy with alpha-1 antitrypsin was administered from 2022-04-26 to 2022-05-14, followed by vedolizumab since 2022-05-16 (three doses). In the absence of response, mesenchymal stromal cell (MSC) therapy was initiated with 60 million cells on 2022-06-11 and 2022-06-18. The patient was discharged on 2022-06-24. A third MSC infusion (60 million cells) was administered on 2022-06-25, and a fourth infusion (60 million cells) on 2022-07-03. 2022-04-11: Bone marrow puncture showed molecular CR and full donor chimerism. 2022-12-24: Persistent cough with sputum; infection was excluded. CRP 0.3 mg/dL. Initiation of FAM regimen (fluticasone inhaled 440 µg BID, azithromycin 250 mg three times per week, montelukast 10 mg daily).
2023-02: Gram-negative sepsis, likely due to translocation/peritonitis. Treated with meropenem 1 g q8h (2023-02-01–2023-02-02) and vancomycin 1.5 g BID (2023-02-01–2023-02-08).
Liposomal amphotericin B 5 mg/kg was initiated due to a positive Aspergillus antigen (2023-02-02–2023-02-27). Chest CTs on 2023-02-03 and 2023-02-14: No pneumonic infiltrates.
2023-09: Sepsis (ICU stay) likely of pneumonic origin. Pseudomonas aeruginosa detected in blood culture (external hospital). Stress cardiomyopathy (EF 37%) diagnosed. No acute treatment indication; managed non-invasively on follow-up. 
2024-01: RSV and influenza A pneumonia, requiring a 10-day inpatient stay. Treated with oseltamivir 75 mg PO BID for 5 days. Suspected bacterial superinfection – piperacillin/tazobactam 4 g/0.5 g q6h added for 7 days.nBAL: Aspergillus antigen positive (2.1); started isavuconazole 200 mg/d after loading with 200 mg q8h for 2 days. C. difficile colitis treated with vancomycin 125 mg q6h for 10 days.
2024-02: Overlap GvHD of skin and joints; started prednisolone and ruxolitinib re-initiated at 10 mg BID. ECP initiated. 2024-04: Oral HSV lesion, treated with valaciclovir 1 g TID. Severe stomatitis developed; ruxolitinib discontinued due to active infection and history of recurrent infections.
2024-05: Initiated methotrexate 10 mg weekly for progressive cGvHD of joints. No clinical response; treatment stopped early due to CKD and lack of effect. 2024-07: Started belumosudil 200 mg/d.
2024-07-11: Prednisolone 40 mg/day restarted, tapered subsequently; pantoprazole added and belumosudil increased to 400 mg/d. 2024-09: Left hip arthroplasty (osteonecrosis).
2024-10-04: Belumosudil discontinued due to lack of response. 2024-10: Prednisolone 20 mg/day restarted for cGvHD of mucous membranes, later tapered. 2024-11-13: Ruxolitinib re-started (10 mg 1-0-1) — however, no improvement and worsening cGvHD observed.
2024-12-11: Sirolimus initiated, with partial clinical response. 2025-02: Sirolimus discontinued due to prolonged infectious complications.

disease_status.valid_request_if: Requests for a concise summary of relapse classification and chimerism status.
disease_status.content: MDS with TP53 mutation (WHO2022), recipient chimerism of 1.7%. Relapsed disease after alloSCT.

special_considerations.valid_request_if: Requests for special considerations that influence therapeutic decisions, including GvHD status, infections, and overall condition.
special_considerations.content: Patient with relapse of high-risk MDS with TP53 mutation, signs of clonal evolution, complicated by severe chronic GvHD not in remission and ongoing active infection and fungual pneumonia. Otherwise relatively young patient in good clinical status.

round_1.working_diagnosis: Acute febrile hypoxemic respiratory illness in an immunocompromised post-alloSCT patient with chronic pulmonary and cutaneous GvHD, highly suspicious for viral and/or invasive fungal pneumonia with concurrent cytopenias requiring further evaluation for possible disease relapse.
differentials: Viral pneumonia, including influenza or other respiratory viruses
Invasive fungal pneumonia, including mold infection with halo sign
Organizing pneumonia associated with chronic GvHD (BOOP pattern)
Cytopenia secondary to infection or GvHD treatment

treatment_plan.action: Provide immediate supportive care with oxygen supplementation and close monitoring of respiratory status and hemodynamics.
Initiate broad-spectrum antibiotic therapy and obtain comprehensive infection cultures (blood, urine, respiratory) to identify pathogens.
Request serum galactomannan and beta-D-glucan to evaluate for invasive fungal infection given the halo sign on imaging.
Perform bone marrow biopsy to evaluate cytopenias and assess for disease relapse versus reactive changes.
Assess baseline organ function, including renal, hepatic, and cardiac status, to guide anti-infective dosing and evaluate overall tolerance for further interventions.
Review current immunosuppressive and prophylactic regimen and consider early involvement of a transplant or GvHD specialist to balance infection control with GvHD activity.

key_findings_quotes: The patient presented with fever (38.6 °C), dry cough, and progressive fatigue.
Chest CT (2025-04-05): Diffuse bilateral ground-glass opacities with one nodular lesion showing a halo sign in the right upper lobe.

round_2.working_diagnosis: Relapsed myelodysplastic syndrome after alloSCT with high-risk cytogenetic abnormalities, complicated by severe bacterial-viral-fungal pulmonary coinfection including influenza A, ESBL-producing Klebsiella pneumoniae with OXA-48 carbapenemase, and probable invasive aspergillosis.
differentials: Relapsed high-risk MDS with complex karyotype after alloSCT
Evolving secondary AML arising from MDS in a post-transplant setting
Reactive dysplasia and marrow suppression in the setting of severe infection and chronic GvHD
Progressive chronic GvHD with secondary marrow dysfunction and clonal evolution

treatment_plan.action: Adjust antibiotic therapy to target OXA-48-producing Klebsiella pneumoniae based on susceptibility results, using last-resort antibiotics as indicated.
Start antiviral therapy with oseltamivir for influenza A infection.
Supplement oxygen via nasal cannula or escalate respiratory support as needed given markedly reduced diffusion capacity.
Maintain antifungal coverage for probable invasive pulmonary aspergillosis given elevated BAL galactomannan.
Integrate bone marrow morphology, flow cytometry, and complex cytogenetics to classify the clonal myeloid disease and confirm relapse.
Perform GvHD staging to assess current severity and guide therapy optimization.
Delay disease-directed cytotoxic therapy until there is at least partial control of the acute infectious process.

key_findings_quotes: Flow cytometry revealed 6% blasts, expressing CD34+, CD117+, CD13+, CD38+, CD123+, and CD45dim.
Bronchoalveolar lavage grew ESBL-producing Klebsiella pneumoniae harboring an OXA-48 carbapenemase gene, and was PCR-positive for influenza A (Ct 17).
Pulmonary function testing demonstrated a markedly reduced diffusion capacity (DLCO 62% predicted) and a moderate obstructive component (FEV₁ 56% predicted).
Antimicrobial susceptibility testing showed resistance to piperacillin/tazobactam, all cephalosporins, and carbapenems.

round_3.working_diagnosis: Relapsed myelodysplastic syndrome with TP53 mutation (WHO 2022) after alloSCT, with clonal evolution on a background of complex cytogenetics and persistent mixed chimerism, complicated by severe steroid-refractory chronic GvHD of skin and joints, and ongoing fungal pneumonia.
differentials: Myelodysplastic syndrome with TP53 mutation (WHO 2022) relapsing after alloSCT with persistent mixed chimerism
Evolving secondary AML arising from high-risk MDS with TP53 mutation and complex karyotype in a post-transplant setting

treatment_plan.action: Initiate azacitidine 75 mg/m² in standard day 1–7 cycles every 28 days as disease-directed therapy for relapsed high-risk MDS with TP53 mutation after alloSCT.
For severe steroid-refractory chronic GvHD of the skin, start axatilimab-csfr 0.3 mg/kg every 14 days within a compassionate use framework.
Intensify antifungal therapy by adding liposomal amphotericin B to the existing regimen to optimize coverage for fungal pneumonia.
Do not pursue donor lymphocyte infusions given the presence of severe chronic GvHD.
Evaluate feasibility of a second allogeneic stem cell transplantation depending on clinical course, infectious control, and patient fitness.
Maintain close multidisciplinary management involving transplant, infectious disease, and GvHD specialists to balance relapse control, infection management, and GvHD activity.

key_findings_quotes: Mutations detected: TP53 (VAF 7%), SF3B1 (VAF 12%), and DNMT3A (VAF 3%).
Relapsed disease with persistent recipient chimerism (1.7%).
Myelodysplastic syndrome (MDS) with TP53 mutation (WHO 2022). Severe chronic GvHD of the skin and joints.
Patient with relapse of high-risk MDS with TP53 mutation, signs of clonal evolution, complicated by severe chronic GvHD not in remission and ongoing active infection and fungual pneumonia.
